title: Rhinanthus × digeneus
taxonomy: Rhinanthus × digeneus is a hybrid species, resulting from the cross between Rhinanthus serotinus and Rhinanthus minor, both belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant, meaning it obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The classification of Rhinanthus × digeneus places it within the genus Rhinanthus, which comprises around 17 species of flowering plants. Rhinanthus × digeneus is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. This hybrid species is commonly found in wet meadows and grasslands in Europe.
uses: Rhinanthus × digeneus, also known as the hybrid monkey flower or yellow rattle, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and pain. However, there is limited scientific evidence to support the effectiveness of Rhinanthus × digeneus in treating these conditions. More research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential uses.

intro: Rhinanthus × digeneus, also known as the cuckoo flower or yellow rattle, is a rare, hybrid plant species that is native to Europe and has been naturalized in some parts of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. The bioactive constituents of Rhinanthus × digeneus include iridoid glycosides, such as aucubin and harpagoside, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create preparations for respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
